item.title: Kone Is Said to Near €29 Billion Deal for Rival TK Elevator
item.description: Kone Oyj is nearing a cash-and-stock deal to acquire TK Elevator, people familiar with the matter said, in what will be one of the biggest-ever private equity exits in Europe.

item.title: China’s Used Cooking Oil Ships to US as War Drives Biofuel Boom
item.description: American imports of used cooking oil from China are set to accelerate as increased US biofuel-blending requirements kick in and the Iran war drives up energy costs, making the feedstock a relative bargain.

item.title: Carney Eyes Building Boom as Oil Prices Lift Revenues in Canada
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney chose to spend a revenue windfall on measures to support building major export-boosting projects, leaving Canada’s fiscal trajectory little changed.

item.title: US Warns of Sanctions Risks for Chinese Refiners of Iranian Oil
item.description: The US warned financial institutions of sanction risks on Chinese oil refineries over ties to Iran, ramping up economic pressure on Tehran and risking tension with Beijing ahead of an expected summit between the country’s leaders.

item.title: Italy’s Eni Expands Venezuela Push With Orinoco Oil Deal
item.description: Eni SpA agreed to restart an oil project in Venezuela’s Orinoco Belt, deepening its presence as US sanctions ease.

item.title: Hollywood Gear Supplier Quixote to Exit Atlanta Operations
item.description: Quixote, a supplier of gear to movie and TV productions, is shutting down its Atlanta-area operations and exiting many of its soundstages in Los Angeles in the latest sign of the slump in US entertainment filming.

item.title: Appalachian Lithium Cache Enough to Power 130 Million EVs, USGS Says
item.description: The Appalachian region of the eastern US holds enough lithium to curb America’s reliance on imports for centuries, according to new research by the US Geological Survey, underscoring domestic resource potential as demand for critical minerals accelerates.

item.title: TD Securities’ Nixon Leaves for National Bank Fixed Income Sales
item.description: Katy Nixon, a director in debt capital markets syndication at TD Securities, is leaving the bank for a fixed income sales role at National Bank of Canada, according to a spokesperson at National Bank.

item.title: Oaktree Capital Accuses BJ’s of Reneging on Tariff Refund Trade
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management LP sued BJ’s Wholesale Club Inc. for allegedly backing out of a deal to sell its rights to around $29 million in tariff refunds for 70 cents on the dollar.

item.title: Jane Street Set to Double Footprint in London Office Move
item.description: Jane Street Group has placed an offer to lease a new London office, a deal that will see the rapidly growing trading firm almost double its footprint in the UK capital.

item.title: Google Allows Pentagon to Use Its AI in Classified Military Work
item.description: Google has reached an agreement with the US Defense Department to allow its artificial intelligence systems to be used for classified military work, according to a Pentagon official, a deal that came together as researchers at the company protested against it.

item.title: Musk Lawyer Says Altman Made ‘Mockery’ of OpenAI Public Mission
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyer argued to a jury Tuesday that the billionaire’s work to develop artificial intelligence technology for the public good was undermined by the co-founders of OpenAI when they turned it into a profit-making venture.

item.title: AI Boom to Triple US Power Equipment Market to $65 Billion
item.description: US spending on power-plant equipment is expected to triple through 2030, primarily driven by the growth of data centers that may account for as much as 40% of total investment, according to a report Tuesday from Wood Mackenzie Ltd.

item.title: Petroperu Needs to Borrow $2 Billion-Plus to Avert Fuel Shortage
item.description: Petroperu needs more than $2 billion of private funding to avoid fuel shortages as Peru’s struggling state-owned oil refiner navigates high global fuel prices due to war in the Middle East.

item.title: Apple Readies Photo-Editing Overhaul With New AI Tools in iOS 27
item.description: Apple Inc. is planning a major overhaul of the built-in photo-editing features for the iPhone, iPad and Mac, leaning heavily on artificial intelligence to better compete with Android devices.

item.title: HSBC Upgrades US Stocks as Focus Shifts to Earnings From War
item.description: Signs that geopolitical risks are easing has shifted the narrative back to fundamentals, prompting HSBC Holdings Plc to lift its view on US equities.

item.title: Dynatrace to Discuss Ideas With Starboard After Firm Takes Stake
item.description: Dynatrace Inc. said it has met with Starboard Value LP and will discuss ideas with the investment firm, which recently took a stake in the tech company and began pushing for changes.

item.title: California Jet Fuel Woes Deepen as Asia Flows Hit Decade Low
item.description: Jet fuel exports from Asia to California are at the lowest point in at least a decade, adding another pressure point to a burgeoning air travel crunch on the West Coast stemming from the war on Iran.

item.title: Budget Fears Weigh on Defense Stocks Ahead of Hegseth Testimony
item.description: Two months after the Iran war began, euphoria around US defense stocks has faded as investors worry a rally that pushed shares to all-time highs went too far, too fast, and that a massive boost to the defense budget is unlikely.

item.title: Franklin Shares Rise as $12.4 Billion Flows Into Alternatives
item.description: Franklin Resources Inc. took in about $12.4 billion for alternative investments in the first three months of the year, as clients withdrew cash from its stock and bond funds.

item.title: Algos Are ‘Dominating’ Buy-Side Currency Trading, Report Says
item.description: Currency traders’ increasing reliance on algorithms is sparking a debate about the merits of traditional banks versus independent firms in the $9.5 trillion-a-day foreign-exchange market, a new report says.

item.title: Traders Protect Against Selloff Even as Oil Climbs Above $110
item.description: Even as Wall Street calls for oil to hold above $100, stockpiles shrivel and the Strait of Hormuz remains all but shut, some traders are buying options to protect against an abrupt deescalation between the US and Iran.

item.title: Goldman’s Flood Says Brace for Near-Term Selloff, Buy Stocks Dip
item.description: US stock investors should brace for a near-term pullback as positioning becomes increasingly stretched and key institutional buyers flip to sellers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s John Flood.

item.title: Blackstone’s QTS Asks Banks for $2 Billion to Guarantee AI Power
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed data center operator QTS is in talks with banks for about $2 billion to help procure electricity, intensifying the race for ever-more creative forms of financing in the industry.

item.title: FDA Plans to Speed Up Drug Trials With Real-Time Data, AI
item.description: The US Food and Drug Administration plans to cut the time it takes drug companies to test new medicines by getting more visibility into clinical trials as they’re underway.

item.title: Cancer Drug Developer Erasca Sinks by Record 55% After Patient Death
item.description: Erasca Inc. shares plunged as much as 55% Tuesday after the cancer-drug developer said one patient withdrew from its clinical trial and later died after suffering severe side effects related to the treatment.

item.title: Lula to Unveil $20 Billion Debt Relief Plan Ahead of Election
item.description: President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva plans to roll out a program to renegotiate more than 100 billion reais ($20 billion) in household debt, adding to a string of measures to support consumption as he seeks to bolster his popularity ahead of Brazil’s October election.

item.title: Markets Downplay War’s Impact on Petrochemicals, Pipeline CEO Says
item.description: Investors are misjudging how much Strait of Hormuz closures could impact global flows of petrochemicals, said Enterprise Products Partners LP Chief Executive Officer Jim Teague, adding to a chorus of industry voices warning about lingering disruptions from the Iran war.

item.title: Wheat Highest Since 2024 as Drought, Fertilizer Costs Hit Supply
item.description: Wheat surged to the highest in nearly two years as drought in the US was pressuring yields at a time soaring fertilizer costs prompted farmers to pare back planting of nutrient-intensive crops such as grains.

item.title: Red-State AGs Call Out Credit-Rating Companies Over ESG Policy
item.description: A group of state attorneys general wrote to the US Securities and Exchange Commission and major credit-rating firms, raising concerns about the use of environmental, social and governance factors in downgrade decisions.
